3-15 I know the place where the Lamanites guard my people whom they have taken prisoners. Release of the Nephite prisoners of war held by the Lamanites, and a safe return of them to their own people, was an objective of prime importance to Moroni. For him to make an exchange with the Lamanites of Lamanitish prisoners he had taken, for Nephite prisoners, man for man, was not a practical thing to do as it would abound only to Ammoron's welfare. It would give him more power for his war-machine…

length one whose name was Laman was found. Formerly he was a servant of the king of the Lamanites whom Amalickiah had caused to be slain. (Alam 47:21-24) As Moroni's plan developed, he sent Laman with a small number of men to contact the guards who were stationed in the City of Gid where the prisoners were located. Here, in the shades of oncoming night, Laman advanced towards the unsuspecting guards. However, they saluted him, thereby challenging him, but Laman knowing the armies' comings and…

24-25 Moroni took possession of the City of Gid. Moroni's objective in taking the City of Gid was fulfilled, and his greater aim in doing so without bloodshed was tantamount to several victories in the field. In doing so he lost no men, but gained the release of the Nephite prisoners many of whom joined his forces, and, also, he took numerous Lamanite prisoners himself. After Moroni received Ammoron's reply and read his justification for attacking the Nephites, he was even angrier. He then decided to get the Nephite prisoners back in his own way. Moroni searched among his men for a descendant of Laman-a Lamanite by birth this…

33-35 Moroni made plans to attack the City Morianton. The Lamanites, notwithstanding their losses in other fields of warfare, were continually strengthening the City Morianton, which was on the east seashore. New supplies backed by added forces from other fields of conflict were making the city an exceedingly strong refuge into which the Lamanites might gather. Whether or not Moroni, despite his plans, attacked the city, we do not know. The Sacred Record makes no mention of it, and surmise is…
